item.title: Quoting Boris Mann 摘要
item.description:
Boris Mann argues that the phrase '11 AI agents' is meaningless, comparing it to saying 'I have 11 spreadsheets' or 'I have 11 browser tabs' to describe one's work. The statement critiques the vague and overused terminology around AI agents, suggesting that such phrasing lacks clarity and fails to convey meaningful information about the actual tasks or functions being performed.
标签: AI, AI-agents, terminology, critique, semantics, productivity, tech-culture

item.title: Software Engineers are Obsolete 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on how AI has democratized software development, making their hard-earned skills feel obsolete as non-developers now build products with AI tools. They contrast this with past experiences, like building a tutorial website and missing out on Silverlight trends. While AI lowers barriers, the author argues that new entrants rely on prompting rather than deep coding, debugging, or maintenance skills. They predict that true engineers who learn the hard way will eventually be valued again, especially for maintaining and fixing software, which AI cannot easily handle. The author concludes by waiting for the inevitable realization that AI cannot solve all problems.
标签: AI, software development, developer obsolescence, gatekeeping, Silverlight, maintenance, prompt engineering, learning, technology trends, personal reflection

item.title: Commenting Guidelines 摘要
item.description:
This article outlines commenting guidelines for a website. Comments may include HTML or Markdown but are sanitized before publication. All submissions are reviewed by the author, who decides whether to publish. Typically published comments add new information, offer insight, provide a viewpoint, report errors, contain humor, or express appreciation. Rants are generally not published to maintain high signal-to-noise ratio. Published comments appear on the article's comment page and a central comment index. Users should not submit sensitive personal data.
标签: commenting, guidelines, moderation, community, meta, website, policy, signal-to-noise
